questions.correct_answer: Yatmadan önce cep telefonu gibi elektronik cihazların kullanılması, melatonin hormonunun salgılanmasını engeller ve stres seviyesini artırır.

questions.text: When a developing fetus or young child is constantly exposed to arsenic, some health issues may develop quickly, but other problems may not show up until later periods in life.
options: Gelişmekte olan bir fetüs ya da küçük bir çocuk sürekli arseniğe maruz kaldığında, bazı sağlık sorunları hızla gelişebilir, fakat diğer problemler hayatın sonraki dönemlerine kadar ortaya çıkmayabilir.
Arseniğe maruz kaldığında bir fetüsün ya da küçük bir çocuğun bazı sağlık sorunları ile karşılaşma ihtimali çok yüksek olabilir, ancak bazıları da bu sorunlarla hayatları boyunca karşılaşmayabilir.
Sürekli olarak arseniğe maruz kalan bir fetüs ya da küçük bir çocukta sağlık sorunları gelişebilir, fakat diğerleri hayatın sonraki dönemlerine kadar ortaya çıkmayabilir.
Gelişmeye devam eden bir fetüs ya da küçük bir çocuk arseniğe maruz kaldığında, bazı sağlık sorunları ile karşılaşabilir, fakat diğerleri hayatının sonuna kadar ortaya çıkmayabilir.

Gelişmekte olan bir fetüsün ya da küçük bir çocuğun bazı sağlık sorunları yaşayabilmesinin sebeplerinden biri sürekli olarak arseniğe maruz bırakılması olabilir, ancak bu sorun yaşayacağı anlamına gelmez.
questions.correct_answer: Gelişmekte olan bir fetüs ya da küçük bir çocuk sürekli arseniğe maruz kaldığında, bazı sağlık sorunları hızla gelişebilir, fakat diğer problemler hayatın sonraki dönemlerine kadar ortaya çıkmayabilir.

questions.correct_answer: Low-cost sensors made from semiconducting plastic could be used to diagnose or monitor various health problems, like neurological diseases.

questions.text: Fırtınaların sıklığında ve şiddetindeki olası değişikliklerin deniz yaşamı üzerinde son derece zararlı etkileri olabilir.
options: Marine life is affected negatively by potential changes in the frequency and intensity of harmful storms.
The frequency and intensity of storms around the world could change the potential cycle of marine life.
Marine life has never been affected by the potential changes in the frequency and intensity of storms.
Potential changes in the frequency and intensity of storms may dramatically affect the diversity of marine life.
Potential changes in the frequency and intensity of storms may have extremely harmful effects on marine life.
questions.correct_answer: Potential changes in the frequency and intensity of storms may have extremely harmful effects on marine life.

questions.text: 9 Yeryüzündeki sıcaklık deniz seviyesinde ortalama 15°C derecedir; ancak bu enleme, yüksekliğe, mevsime ve günün saatine göre değişiklik gösterir.
options: Latitude, elevation, season, and time of day determine the temperature on Earth, but it usually averages 15°C at sea level.
The temperature on Earth is measured 15°C on average at sea level; however, it may vary as a result of seasonal and timely changes.
The temperature on Earth averages 15°C at sea level; however, it varies according to latitude, elevation, season, and time of day.
The temperature at sea level averages 15°C; however, it may vary according to latitude, elevation, season, and time of day.
The temperature on Earth varies according to latitude, elevation, and season; but it is usually about 15°C at sea level during the day.
questions.correct_answer: The temperature on Earth averages 15°C at sea level; however, it varies according to latitude, elevation, season, and time of day.

questions.text: İnsanlar günlük yaşamlarında bir evin duvarları için ne kadar boyanın gerekli olduğunu hesaplamak gibi işler için geometri formüllerine ihtiyaç duyarlar.
options: People need geometric formulas in everyday life for tasks, such as calculating how much paint is necessary for the walls of a house.
In order to carry out certain tasks in everyday life, like calculating how much paint will be needed for the walls of a house, people need certain geometric formulas.
Geometric formulas are required for tasks in everyday life, such as calculating how much paint is necessary for all the walls of a house.
People need to know geometric formulas in order to carry out tasks, such as calculating how much paint they will need to cover the walls of a house.
It is necessary for people to know geometric formulas when they perform tasks, such as calculating how much paint is necessary for the walls of a house.
questions.correct_answer: People need geometric formulas in everyday life for tasks, such as calculating how much paint is necessary for the walls of a house.

questions.text: 1920’lere kadar, radyasyonla ilgili ço ğu çalışma, radyoaktif mineraller ya da x ışınları gibi insan yapımı elektromanyetik radyasyon ile ilgiliydi.
options: Until the 1920s, most studies of radiation were concerned with radioactive minerals other than electromagnetic radiation, such as x-rays.
Until the 1920s, many scientists preferred to conduct research into radioactive minerals or electromagnetic radiation, such as x-rays.
Radioactive minerals or man-made electromagnetic radiation, such as x-rays, were the main fields of study until the 1920s.
Until the 1920s, most studies of radiation were related to radioactive minerals or man-made electromagnetic radiation, such as x-rays.
Until the 1920s, studies of radiation were generally related to radioactive minerals and man-made electromagnetic radiation like x-rays.
questions.correct_answer: Until the 1920s, most studies of radiation were related to radioactive minerals or man-made electromagnetic radiation, such as x-rays.

questions.text: Bir inşaat mühendisi, genellikle diğer mühendislik alanlarının temel bilgisine ihtiyaç duyar, çünkü çoğu mühendislik konuları birbiriyle bağlantılıdır.
options: Most engineering issues are generally interrelated; therefore, civil engineers need knowledge of other basic engineering fields.
A civil engineer usually needs basic knowledge of other engineering fields, because most engineering issues are interrelated.
A civil engineer usually needs certain basic knowledge of other engineering fields because it is essential to deal with related issues.
A basic knowledge of various engineering fields is essential for a civil engineer as many engineering issues seem interrelated.
The basic aim of civil engineers is to have basic knowledge of other engineering fields, as they apply it on an interrelated issue.
questions.correct_answer: A civil engineer usually needs basic knowledge of other engineering fields, because most engineering issues are interrelated.

questions.text: bütünlüğünü sağlamak için boş bırakılan yerlere  getirilebilecek cümleyi bulunuz. Many bacteria are anaerobic; that is, they do not consume oxygen and, indeed, are opposed to it. -------. The atmosphere at that time was probably a mixture of nitrogen with gases such as carbon monoxide and water vapor, or perhaps methane. These primitive bacteria needed some source of energy to drive their biochemical processes, and some researchers believe they may have at first found this source in the heat and chemical energy of undersea volcanoes.
options: - 59. sorularda, parçada anlam bütünlüğünü sağlamak için boş bırakılan yerlere  getirilebilecek cümleyi bulunuz. Many bacteria are anaerobic; that is, they do not consume oxygen and, indeed, are opposed to it. -------. The atmosphere at that time was probably a mixture of nitrogen with gases such as carbon monoxide and water vapor, or perhaps methane. These primitive bacteria needed some source of energy to drive their biochemical processes, and some researchers believe they may have at first found this source in the heat and chemical energy of undersea volcanoes. 54. All living organisms need oxygen to sustain life in their surroundings
When life began, over 3.8 billion years ago, the first cells were also anaerobic
At an early stage in biological life, sunlight was the only source of energy
There is no known geological process that can maintain a high level of oxygen
A planet with an oxygen blanket could support life but does not necessarily do so
questions.correct_answer: When life began, over 3.8 billion years ago, the first cells were also anaerobic

questions.text: The brain receives a constant stream of information as electrical impulses from neurons in the sense organs. -------. If it is irrelevant, it is allowed to fade away, and we are not conscious of it. However, if it is novel or important, the brain ampliﬁes the signals, causing them to be represented in various regions. If this activity is sustained for long enough, it will result in a conscious experience. In some cases, thoughts are taken one step further, and the brain instructs the body to act on them, by sending signals to the muscles to make them contract.
options: No one knows exactly how electrical activity in the brain turns into experience
Unconscious brain processing, however, guides and sometimes initiates actions
The ﬁrst thing it does is to determine whether the information requires attention
They respond to stimuli in much the same way: they generate electrical signals
The primary task of the brain is to maintain the whole body in an optimal state
questions.correct_answer: The ﬁrst thing it does is to determine whether the information requires attention

questions.text: Buildings that can change shape in a split second or fold into a bag. It sounds futuristic, but the technology is within reach, since scientists from Harvard University have managed to develop a material that can change size, shape, and volume on command. The very strong material can also change its hardness to become either bendable or completely inflexible. -------. The new material is, however, not made of paper, but rather of the artificial material polyethylene, which is folded into a cube with two missing sides and combined with other, completely identical cubes.
options: The scientists were inspired by the traditional Japanese art form of origami, or the art of paper folding
The material will also react to electric impulses, so it can more easily change shape in the field
The paper house invented by the scientists can be folded along all edges, and it changes volume
It can be used for a number of different purposes, such as building refugee camps or even spacecraft
It changes shape by means of pressurized air, which forces the building blocks to change shape
questions.correct_answer: The scientists were inspired by the traditional Japanese art form of origami, or the art of paper folding

questions.text: 1 Cyberattacks will become more common in the years ahead. That is not just a problem for big companies and governments: every person who uses modern technology is a target. At risk is not abstract data or “secrets”. Cybersecurity is now about protecting things, infrastructures, and processes that support modern life. Governments and technology companies cannot secure cyberspace alone. It will take a collective immune system to do the job. Individuals also have a role to play. -------.
options: In the coming years, cyberattacks will almost certainly increase, and that will be a serious problem for all of us
Recently, criminals accessed the data of more than 80 million customers of the health insurance company called Anthem
Now that everyone is connected in some way to cyberspace—through phones, laptops, corporate networks—we are all defenseless
Every network-connected person needs to support the immune system by practicing the cyber equivalent of personal hygiene
For instance, cybercriminals stole the credit-card information and personal data of millions of people from companies
questions.correct_answer: Every network-connected person needs to support the immune system by practicing the cyber equivalent of personal hygiene

questions.text: One colony of bees can carry pollen between 300 million flowers in a single day, and hives are transported between fields across the world to fertilize 70% of our most widely consumed crops. They also help plants to produce better crops, increase yields, and trigger fruits, nuts, and seeds to grow larger. It is not just fruits and vegetables that would go missing from our kitchens if the bees died, either. -------.
options: However, the disappearance of bees would not be the end of food altogether
Moreover, a single bee makes one twelfth of a teaspoon of honey during its lifetime
Therefore, we need these little creatures to keep our supermarkets stocked
Flies, birds, moths, and butterflies are all important pollinators, too
Our livestock also feed on crops like alfalfa and clover, which bees pollinate
questions.correct_answer: Our livestock also feed on crops like alfalfa and clover, which bees pollinate

questions.text: For decades, genetics taught us a simple truth: Each cell in our body, at some point in its development, contains the blueprint that tells us how to grow. -------. A group of researchers from Harvard University now says that tiny bioelectric signals surging through and among our cells act as an instruction to kick-start gene expression. These signals point cells in the right direction as they start to grow into a heart or a hair follicle, and influence the shape and function of the body.
options: Moreover, it is quite similar to a human body
A cell consists of cytoplasm and a nucleus
These scientists could not find these signals
Therefore, the shape of our body matters
However, that might not be the whole story
questions.correct_answer: However, that might not be the whole story

questions.text: 2 60 - 65. sorularda, cümleler sırasıyla okunduğunda parçanın anlam bütünlüğünü bozan cümleyi bulunuz. (I) One of the most important distinctions made by embryologists was the difference between analogy and homology. (II) Both terms refer to structures that appear to be similar. (III) Homologous structures are those organs whose underlying similarity is the result of their being from a common ancestral structure. (IV) Embryology is a subdivision of anatomy that deals with developmental changes that occur before birth. (V) On the other hand, analogous structures are those whose similarity comes from their performing a similar function, rather than their arising from a common ancestor.
options: I
II
III
IV
V
questions.correct_answer: IV

questions.text: (I) Technological enthusiasm pertains to the ideal of wanting to develop new technological possibilities and take up technological challenges. (II) The inherent danger of technological advances lies in the possible negative effects of technology and the relevant social constraints. (III) This is an ideal that motivates many engineers. (IV) It is fitting that Samuel Florman refers to this as “the existential pleasures of engineering”. (V) One good example of technological enthusiasm is the development of Google Earth, a program with which, via the Internet, it is possible to zoom in on the Earth’s surface.
options: I
II
III
IV
V
questions.correct_answer: II

questions.text: (I) Planets that orbit other stars are called exoplanets. (II) The surface of Ganymede, the largest moon in the solar system, appears to have a dual personality. (III) Some regions are dark and densely cratered, suggesting that they look much the same today as they did billions of years ago. (IV) Other regions are light-colored with very few craters, suggesting that liquid water has recently erupted and refrozen. (V) Moreover, magnetic field data indicate that Ganymede, like Europa, could have a subsurface ocean of liquid water.
options: I
II
III
IV
V
questions.correct_answer: I

questions.text: 3 (I) When prospectors discovered the first underground helium reserve in 1903, they deemed the gas useless because it was not flammable and could not be sold as fuel. (II) Earth might generate helium gas naturally by underground radioactivity. (III) Over a century later, liquid helium has become an invaluable coolant for MRI scanners, and the gas also has applications in nuclear power. (IV) Yet supplies on Earth are finite and unreliable because reserves have only been found accidentally during petroleum exploration. (V) Now, experts have discovered one of the world’s largest helium fields, this time in the Tanzanian Rift Valley, on purpose.
options: I
II
III
IV
V
questions.correct_answer: II

questions.text: (I) The North Pole is one of the most remote places on the planet. (II) Despite the harsh environment, there is contention over its ownership as there could be rich natural resources beneath the ice. (III) To ensure its presence in the Arctic, the Canadian government has started working on drones capable of surviving the harsh climatic conditions. (IV) This is because explorers have died in the sub-zero temperatures of the most northerly point on the planet, and drones would be likely to take their place. (V) Additionally, as the Arctic is impacted by the climate change, the natural gas and oil will be easier to extract.
options: I
II
III
IV
V
questions.correct_answer: V

questions.text: (I) The fast increasing industrialization was based on import of cheap raw materials and export of finished products. (II) This was only possible if the transport systems kept up with demand and could offer reasonable prices.   (III)  With these innovations, Britain produced the best iron in the highest quantities by the late 18 th century. (IV)  The canal system was relatively cheap but time-consuming. (V) Soon two new transport modes were invented to cope with the demands of the Industrial Revolution: Steamship and Railways.
options: I
II
III
IV
V
questions.correct_answer: III

questions.text: 4 66 - 68. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. The call for all students to learn computer programming is growing louder. Some believe that computer science instruction in public schools can close achievement gaps among socioeconomic groups and help students compete with those in other countries. Supporters of this idea include business leaders who will employ the next generation of engineers and programmers, as well as government officials who are worried about the country’s competitiveness in computer programming. However, others argue that coding for all students will create logistical challenges, including a shortage of teachers, the absence of an agreed-on curriculum, and inequalities in students’ access to computers. Some critics also argue that coding represents a narrow technical focus and that business leaders who will benefit from this are pushing it on schools. A potential middle ground involves teaching “computational thinking,” In other words, there is a need to develop habits of mind that include breaking down a problem, designing systems, and running small experiments to see which approaches fail and which succeed. The text is mainly about -------.
options: controversial ideas about teaching computer programming in public schools
why every child should learn computer programming at an early age
new job opportunities created by the introduction of computer programming
the negative effects of coding and programming on school curricula
the reasons for supporting computer programming courses at schools
questions.correct_answer: controversial ideas about teaching computer programming in public schools

questions.text: It is clear from the text that -------.
options: some political leaders are worried because they think competitiveness in computer programming may cause problems between countries
it is necessary to have an adequate number of qualified teachers and a good curriculum to start teaching computer programming in schools
some people may lose their jobs in the future if computer programming becomes available to every individual
governments need to step in to overcome inequalities in students’ access to computers
business leaders have some serious doubts about the benefits of teaching computer programming in schools
questions.correct_answer: it is necessary to have an adequate number of qualified teachers and a good curriculum to start teaching computer programming in schools

questions.text: From the text, we can infer that -------.
options: young people need to be encouraged to share their ideas about computer programming and their future careers
business leaders and government officials will soon come to an agreement about computer programming education
the disadvantages of computational thinking outweigh its advantages
the future of certain businesses depend solely on computer programming
computational thinking may be an answer to the ongoing debate about computer programming education
questions.correct_answer: computational thinking may be an answer to the ongoing debate about computer programming education

questions.text: 5 69 - 71. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. Last year, an international team of astronomers published a shocking new report on the extent of light pollution on Earth. The bottom line for those who care about the night sky is significant: Some 80% of the globe is adversely affected by night-time light pollution, and the problem in North America is growing worse by 6% each year. More than 99% of Europeans do not see a dark night sky. By percentage of population, Singapore is the most light-polluted country on Earth, followed by Kuwait, Qatar, and the United Arab Emirates. Africa is home to the top 10 least light-polluted countries. Such a pollution creates a significant problem; disturbingly, almost one-third of the world’s population cannot fully see the Milky Way. Light pollution is also amateur astronomers' worst enemy as this is not a hobby you do in your basement. That is why an institute is offering a place for amateur astronomers to go, called Dark Sky New Mexico. The site offers world-class dark skies within a four-hour drive of major southwestern cities. The institute provides remote observing and imaging from this pristine site. We understand from the text that -------.
options: Dark Sky New Mexico is an unspoiled place not affected by night-time light pollution
the group is offering Dark Sky New Mexico because it is the nearest observing site
amateur astronomers need somewhere closer to the sky to observe the planets
places like Dark Sky New Mexico are popular sites among amateur astronomers
African countries are also severely affected by the threat of night-time light pollution
questions.correct_answer: Dark Sky New Mexico is an unspoiled place not affected by night-time light pollution

questions.text: It is stated in the text that -------.
options: the extent of light pollution is growing dramatically each year only in densely populated countries
the report issued last year also offers some solutions for the problem of night-time pollution
night-time light pollution has some severe health effects on people in big cities
the rate of night-time light pollution in Europe is far higher than the world average rate
African countries do not care about the night sky as they have very few amateur astronomers
questions.correct_answer: the rate of night-time light pollution in Europe is far higher than the world average rate

questions.text: It is clear from the text that -------.
options: sites offering world-class dark skies are fewer in number if you travel from Europe to America
government have started to make large investments to end light pollution
it is impossible for about 30% of the world population to see the Milky Way
major southwestern cities are not affected by light pollution as they are close to New Mexico
the number of amateur astronomers is increasing despite the threat of light pollution
questions.correct_answer: it is impossible for about 30% of the world population to see the Milky Way

questions.text: 6 72 - 74. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. Careful study of the galaxy’s rotation reveals one of the greatest mysteries in science. Stars at different distances from the galactic center orbit at different speeds, and we can learn how mass is distributed in the galaxy by measuring these speeds. Such studies indicate that the stars in the disk of the galaxy represent only the ”tip of the iceberg” compared to the mass of the entire galaxy. Most of the mass of the galaxy seems to be located outside the visible disk, in what we call the halo. We do not know the nature of this mass, but we call it dark matter because we have not detected any light coming from it. Studies of other galaxies suggest that they are also made mostly of dark matter, which means this mysterious matter must signiﬁcantly outweigh the ordinary matter that makes up planets and stars. An even more mysterious dark energy seems to make up much of the total energy content of the universe. According to the text, measuring the speed of stars is necessary to -------.
options: measure the distances from the galactic center
understand the distribution of mass in the galaxy
calculate and compare the mass of the whole galaxy
prove why the previous physics theories were wrong
show that interstellar travel is possible
questions.correct_answer: understand the distribution of mass in the galaxy

questions.text: It can be understood from the text that -------.
options: the study of the ordinary matter may help scientists make predictions about the entire galaxy
we still do not know whether dark matter can be found in every galaxy or not
much of the total energy content of the universe comes from the ordinary matter
most of the mass lies unseen in the disk of the galaxy that surrounds it entirely
the stars in the disk of the galaxy constitute only a small proportion of the mass of the entire galaxy
questions.correct_answer: the stars in the disk of the galaxy constitute only a small proportion of the mass of the entire galaxy

questions.text: The text is mainly about -------.
options: the results of scientific studies concerning the distribution of mass in the galaxy
the scientific discoveries of the energy sources of the universe
how the Sun and other stars orbit the center of the galaxy
why billions of galaxies in the universe move relative to one another
the consequences of not knowing the nature of dark matter
questions.correct_answer: the results of scientific studies concerning the distribution of mass in the galaxy

questions.text: 7 75 - 77. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. Towards the end of the 1960s, the Ford Motor Company, one of the world’s largest car manufacturers, was gradually losing market share. In 1968, President Lee Iacocca decided that a small cheap car had to be designed quickly. This was to become the Ford Pinto. The decision was to put it onto the market for less than $2,000 in 1970. This was a very competitive price, but the production schedule for the car’s development was rushed. At the time, car development normally required around 43 months. Only 24 months were reserved for the Ford Pinto. Because the Pinto had to cost a maximum of $2,000, a radical design was selected in which styling took precedence over engineering design. The safety aspect of the design did not receive sufficient priority. There was no experience with small cars within the company at all. Later it was found that the gear construction in the rear axles was situated in a way that would puncture the gas tank in the event of a collision. We understand from the text that -------.
options: the engineers made careful calculations long before they started to work on the new model
the price of the Ford Pinto would be no less than $2,000 on the market
the Ford Motor Company refused to be rushed into designing the Ford Pinto
the Ford Motor Company produced a small car in order to compete with its rivals
since the Ford Pinto was a small car, the duration of its production took less time than expected
questions.correct_answer: the Ford Motor Company produced a small car in order to compete with its rivals

questions.text: It is clear from the text that -------.
options: the new model was supposed to be designed within 43 months
the appearance and engineering of Ford Pinto were equally valued
the design of the new model car was uncommon at that time
the primary aim in designing the new car model was its safety
the production of the new model car took more than two years
questions.correct_answer: the design of the new model car was uncommon at that time

questions.text: According to the text, -------.
options: the gear mechanism operated efficiently
it took longer than average to design the new model
the company produced several models like the Pinto before
safety issues were overlooked to an extent in the new design
the new model became an immediate success on the market
questions.correct_answer: safety issues were overlooked to an extent in the new design

questions.text: 8 78 - 80. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. Toward the end of the 19th century, it became clear that atoms are not indivisible. The existence of characteristic atomic spectra of elements suggested that atoms have an internal structure, and J. J. Thomson’s discovery of the negatively charged electron in 1897 showed that atoms could be broken down into charged particles. Rutherford’s experiments in 1910-1911 revealed that an atom’s positive charge resides in a small, dense nucleus. In 1919, Rutherford made an additional discovery: When alpha particles are ﬁred into nitrogen, one of the products is hydrogen gas. He reasoned that the hydrogen nucleus is a constituent of the nuclei of heavier atoms, such as nitrogen, and that a collision with a fast-moving alpha particle can dislodge one of those hydrogen nuclei. Thus, the hydrogen nucleus is an elementary particle, to which Rutherford gave the name proton. The following decade saw the blossoming of quantum mechanics, including the Schrödinger equation. It is owing to all these physicists that today we are on our way to understanding the principles that underlie atomic structure. It can be understood from the text that -------.
options: Thomson was able to fill the gap in physics by building on the theory of Rutherford
it was not until Thomson’s finding that atoms were thought to be divided into particles
Rutherford’s findings regarding the atomic structure refuted Thomson’s theories
Rutherford succeeded in locating the nucleus near the positively-charged particle
Thomson is usually credited with the discovery of a subatomic particle called the proton
questions.correct_answer: it was not until Thomson’s finding that atoms were thought to be divided into particles

questions.text: It can be understood from the text that -------.
options: Thomson and Rutherford studied atoms independently and had contradictory results
neither Thomson’s nor Rutherford’s experiments provided guidance to later researchers
developments in the field of physics cannot be attributed to the works of a single scientist
Rutherford had to modify his experiment to validate his results regarding the effects of nitrogen
when physicists raised general interest in quantum mechanics, the structure of the atom was completely understood
questions.correct_answer: developments in the field of physics cannot be attributed to the works of a single scientist

questions.text: The text is mainly about -------.
options: the conflicting ideas of major physicists
common misconceptions of atomic structure
an analysis of the studies of Thomson and Rutherford
the description of how an atom splits into particles
the origins of the modern understanding of atomic structure
questions.correct_answer: the origins of the modern understanding of atomic structure
